data.title: ZeroLend, a crypto protocol, ceases operations due to being ‘no longer sustainable’ as inactive blockchains result in losses - Founder Ryker.
data.description: The founder of ZeroLend, under the pseudonym "Ryker," has reported that multiple blockchains utilized by the lending protocol have become "inactive." This situation has resulted in instances where the platform has been functioning at a financial loss.

data.title: Steak 'n Shake reports a significant sales increase in 9 months due to Bitcoin transactions funneled into corporate reserve.
data.description: The popular fast-food chain has reported a significant increase in sales thanks to accepting bitcoin payments. This move has led to a rise in the company's corporate reserve as it continues to accumulate cryptocurrency receipts.

data.title: Anthropic teams up with Infosys to launch AI agents in telecom and finance, using Claude models on Infosys Topaz. (Read More)
data.description: Anthropic and Infosys have recently joined forces to develop enterprise AI agents tailored for industries with strict regulations. The collaboration involves utilizing Claude models and Infosys Topaz platform to enhance the development process. This partnership aims to combine Anthropic's expertise in AI with Infosys' advanced technology, offering innovative solutions for regulated industries. By leveraging these tools, the companies plan to create sophisticated AI agents that meet the specific needs of businesses operating in highly regulated sectors.

data.title: Truecaller AB reports a 14% decrease in net sales to SEK 450.9 million in 2025; aims for next growth phase.
data.description: In its year-end report for January-December 2025, Truecaller, the top global platform for contact verification and blocking unwanted communication, disclosed a 14% drop in net sales to SEK 450.9 million (from 522.8 million). Adjusted for constant currencies, the decline...

data.title: Crypto sentiment reaches extreme fear as Matrixport suggests possible bottom. Bitcoin sentiment at 4-year lows; historic oversold signals noted.
data.description: Analysts are pointing out historic oversold signals and potential seller exhaustion as Bitcoin sentiment drops to levels not seen in the past four years. This indicates a significant decrease in positive perception towards Bitcoin.

data.title: Crypto.com expands AI services, following ISO/IEC 42001 certification. AI is becoming a core business line, reflecting industry trend.
data.description: Crypto.com is enhancing its AI offerings as a key focus among its three core business activities, in response to the increasing adoption of this technology across the industry.

data.title: Ethereum overtaken by Polygon in daily fees as prediction market sees surge in activity.
data.description: In the latest report, it has been revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a record low of 3.8%. This decrease marks a significant drop from the previous rate of 4.2% and is the lowest it has been in over a decade. The report also shows that the economy has added over 250,000 new jobs in the last month alone, surpassing all expectations. This surge in job creation has been attributed to growth in various sectors, including technology, healthcare, and manufacturing. Additionally, the average hourly wage has seen a notable increase of 4.5% compared to last year. Experts predict that this trend is likely to continue, indicating a strong and healthy job market in the foreseeable future.

data.title: Beactica and KU Leuven get EUR 2.5M to develop orphan drug BEA-17 for clinical trials.
data.description: In Uppsala, Sweden on February 17, 2026, Beactica Therapeutics AB, a precision medicine company, revealed receiving a EUR 2.5 million grant from the European Innovation Council (EIC). The grant, in partnership with leading glioblastoma researchers at KU Leuven, aims to further their research initiatives.

data.title: ZeroLend, a crypto protocol, closes due to sustainability issues. Founder blames inactive blockchains, causing losses.
data.description: The founder of ZeroLend, who goes by the name "Ryker", revealed that the lending protocol is facing challenges as several blockchains it operates on are currently "inactive." This has resulted in periods where the protocol experienced losses due to the inactivity of these blockchains.

data.title: Trifork introduces Danish data/AI solution as US hyperscaler alternative for digital sovereignty. Danish authorities seek greater control.
data.description: Trifork has introduced a Danish sovereign data and AI solution in Aarhus on 17th February 2026. Public authorities in Denmark are encountering issues due to reliance on a few major foreign technology firms, leading to restricted control over data and essential digital infrastructure. A report from January 2026 highlights the challenges faced by public authorities in embracing digital alternatives. The focus is on achieving enhanced digital sovereignty by offering more choices, control, and the flexibility to change service providers, rather than complete detachment.

data.title: BW LPG Limited to release Q4 2025 Financial Report on March 3, 2026. Largest VLGC fleet owner. CEO to lead Earnings Presentation.
data.description: Singapore-based BW LPG Limited, possessing the biggest fleet of Very Large Gas Carriers globally, is set to release its Q4 2025 Financial Report on Tuesday, March 3, 2026, at around 07:00 CET/ 01:00 EST/ 14:00 SGT. The Company's OSE ticker is "BWLPG.OL" and NYSE ticker is "BWLP". An Earnings Presentation will follow the financial report, conducted by CEO Kristian Sørensen and Samanth.

data.title: Unitree Robotics presents "Cyber Real Kung Fu" at China Media Group's 2026 Spring Festival Gala with G1 and H2 robots.
data.description: In Hangzhou, China, on February 17th, 2026, Unitree Robotics participated for the third time as the official robot partner of the 2026 Spring Festival Gala of China Media Group. The company showcased its G1 and H2 humanoid robots during the event, emphasizing innovation and technology on a grand stage as the new year commenced.

data.title: Basilea exceeded 2025 financial goals with 11% total revenue increase, 15% royalty income rise and tripled net cash position.
data.description: Basilea Pharmaceutica Ltd, a biopharma company, announced its financial results for 2025, with a 11% increase in total revenue to CHF 232.4 million, driven by a 15% rise in royalty income to CHF 111.6 million. The company's net cash position tripled to CHF 86.9 million. Investments in research and development reached CHF 105.9 million, focusing on advancing drugs like fosmanogepix. Basilea aims for double-digit revenue and profit growth in 2026. They are well-positioned for growth with plans to bring new drugs to market and expand their commercial assets. Basilea's CEO highlighted their transformation towards severe infection treatments, aiming for long-term growth and addressing unmet medical needs. A conference call and webcast is scheduled for 4 p.m. (CET) to discuss their financial results and future prospects.
